title: peucedanum palustre
intro: Peucedanum palustre, also known as marsh caraway or marsh parsley, is a perennial herb belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds that can help soothe digestive issues, reduce pain, and fight off infections. The health benefits of Peucedanum palustre include its potential to alleviate symptoms of bloating, cramps, and diarrhea, as well as its ability to support the immune system. This plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote overall well-being and digestive health.
